name: mathjax-rendering
description: Render mathematical formulas in Obsidian using LaTeX/MathJax syntax. Use when writing equations, matrices, integrals, summations, or any mathematical notation in Obsidian notes.
capabilities: Write inline and block math equations using LaTeX syntax
Create matrices with various bracket styles (pmatrix, bmatrix, vmatrix)
Use Greek letters and mathematical symbols in formulas
Format integrals, sums, limits, and derivatives correctly
Apply decorations like hats, bars, vectors, and overbraces
Align multi-line equations and create conditional definitions
author: BitYoungjae
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing LaTeX/MathJax reference tables. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by the scanner misidentifying LaTeX command syntax (like \alpha, \equiv) as Ruby/shell backtick execution and cryptographic patterns. This skill has no executable code, no file system access, no network operations, and no command execution capability. It only provides static reference content for mathematical notation.
valueStatement: Obsidian uses MathJax to render mathematical notation but the LaTeX syntax is difficult to remember. This skill provides quick reference tables for fractions, matrices, integrals, Greek letters, and all common math symbols so you can write professional equations in your notes.
limitations: Does not execute or verify LaTeX code - provides reference only
Does not generate rendered images or preview output
Limited to MathJax-supported LaTeX subset supported by Obsidian
No troubleshooting or error correction for malformed equations

useCases.title: Create Math Notes
useCases.description: Write lecture notes with properly formatted equations for calculus, linear algebra, and statistics.

useCases.title: Document Formulas
useCases.description: Include complex mathematical notation in research documentation and technical papers within Obsidian.

useCases.title: Build Math Content
useCases.description: Create educational materials with clear mathematical expressions and step-by-step derivations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Equation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to write a fraction, square root, and Greek letter in math mode

promptTemplates.title: Matrix Display
promptTemplates.prompt: Show a 3x3 identity matrix using bmatrix environment with proper syntax

promptTemplates.title: Calculus Notation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a definite integral from 0 to infinity with proper spacing

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Line Proof
promptTemplates.prompt: Demonstrate the binomial expansion using aligned environment with proper alignment

bestPractices: Use $...$ for inline equations and $$...$$ for display equations
Wrap multi-character exponents and subscripts in braces like x^{2n}
Use \left( and \right) for auto-sizing delimiters around fractions
antiPatterns: Forgetting to escape special characters outside math mode breaks rendering
Using unescaped brackets in Markdown causes syntax errors
Missing braces around multi-character subscripts like x_ij instead of x_{ij}

faq.question: Does this work with Obsidian mobile apps?
faq.answer: Yes. Obsidian mobile fully supports MathJax with the same LaTeX syntax as desktop.

faq.question: What LaTeX packages are supported?
faq.answer: Obsidian supports amsmath, amsfonts, cancel and other common packages.

faq.question: Can I number equations automatically?
faq.answer: Use the equation environment for numbered equations.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill is read-only documentation and does not access your vault data.

faq.question: Why do my equations not render?
faq.answer: Common issues include missing dollar signs and unclosed braces.

faq.question: How is this different from LaTeX editors?
faq.answer: This skill provides MathJax syntax optimized for Obsidian.
